groups.context_description: God-fearer as inner-being identity — those characterised by reverence for God as their defining mark, recipients of divine attention and blessing
Fear as inner state of anxiety or dread directed at human threats — inner person unsettled by danger, opposition, or uncertain outcomes
Fear of God as foundational inner-being orientation — commanded disposition of the whole person from which obedience, love, and faithfulness flow
God-fearer as inner-being identity — divine favour, belonging, and blessing attached to the person characterised by reverence for God
Awe at God's acts and nature — inner-being response of wonder and reverence to divine power, holiness, and mighty deeds
Fear of God expressed in communal and relational life — reverence shaping conduct toward others and covenantal faithfulness
Inner state of terror or startled fright — inner person overwhelmed and destabilised by sudden or threatening encounters
Act of terrifying — inner-being impact of overwhelming authoritative force, in context of pastoral authority and its appropriate limits
Inner state of intimidation — inner person unsettled by hostile opposition, contrasted with inner steadiness of those whose salvation is secure
Inner state of dismay — inner person overwhelmed, destabilised, and paralysed by threat, shame, or collapse of what was trusted
Commanded absence of dismay — inner steadiness called for in the face of threat, opposition, or cosmic instability
Inner terror induced by divine action — overwhelming of the inner person by God's power or judgment, including awe of covenantal reverence
Reverent awe as fitting inner disposition for worship of God
Term names contentment as a learned inner disposition — the acquired capacity of the inner person to remain settled and sufficient regardless of external circumstances
Term names the inner state of contentment and sufficiency — the condition of the person whose inner life is settled and satisfied, neither grasping nor anxious, freed for generous action
What is precious/desirable as the object of inner-being longing or delight
The loss or desecration of what is precious — inner-being grief
Pleasantness in relationship with God — the delight of dwelling in God's presence and praise
Pleasantness in human relationships and wisdom
Pleasantness/favor as the quality of divine and human goodness that refreshes the soul
Refusing the delicacies of the wicked — inner-being discipline against enticement
Pleasant things as misplaced inner-being trust — delight in what is not God
God's delight in or non-delight in persons, sacrifices, and ways
Human delight in God, his law, or his ways — the inner orientation of the righteous
Human desire/will in various contexts — including desire for what is wrong
Divine delight expressed as a new name — God's inner joy in his people
God's declaration of delight in his Son and Servant
God's pleasure or displeasure as the criterion of acceptance
Human inner willingness and readiness — being content or willing
Joy as the inner-being overflow of delight — exultation, gladness, exceeding rejoicing
Pleasure as the competing inner orientation — desires that war within and displace the good
Delight/delicacy as what gives inner satisfaction or is forcibly taken
Voluptuous pleasure-loving as an inner orientation of self-sufficient pride
Delighting in God's goodness — inner joy in divine provision
Delight/delicacy as what gives inner satisfaction or is forcibly taken
Tender refinement — the inner disposition of delicacy and its vulnerability to collapse
Delighting in God's word and finding consolation — inner-being rest in God's statutes
Gladly receiving — the inner posture of willing, pleased reception
Extreme gladness — inner joy that delights in weakness and self-giving
God's delight in or non-delight in persons, sacrifices, and ways
Human delight in God, his law, or his ways — the inner orientation of the righteous
Human desire/will in various contexts — including desire for what is wrong
Delighting in the Lord — the inner-being orientation of finding satisfaction in God
Deep delight as a settled inner orientation — the law as delight and God's heartfelt attachment
Blinding of inner perception — the hardening of heart and eye against understanding
Luxury and delight as the inner orientation toward what is deeply pleasing
Divine acceptance of offerings — God's inner-being pleasure in what is offered
Divine pleasure in persons/character — God's inner delight in the righteous
Human acceptance/favor — the inner-being receipt of divine or human favour
Enjoyment/delight as an inner-being experience — its pursuit or its denial
Pleasantness as an inner-being quality — what is genuinely good and delightful to the soul
Delight and its loss — where delicacies represent the satisfaction of the inner person
The Sabbath as delight — inner orientation of willing, joyful rest
The name Hephzibah — divine delight encoded as identity
Delight in the law of God in the inner being

Term names the act and habitual inner posture of giving thanks to God — the reorientation of the inner being toward gratitude as a response to grace received, the absence of which marks the corruption of the inner person, and the presence of which sanctifies all conduct and circumstances
Term names thankfulness as an inner-being disposition of the renewed person — commanded alongside inner peace as the hallmark of the called community

Term names thanksgiving as an inner-being quality and communal practice — the disposition of gratitude that sanctifies all things received, arises from grace multiplied, and constitutes a core element of the inner life of prayer
Term names the triumphant inner cry — the cry of joy or its conspicuous absence marking the inner condition of the person
Term names the shout or song of deliverance — the joyful cry that arises from rescue and divine protection
Term names communal joy in the celebration of God's provision and restoration
Term names the joyful shout whose absence marks a condition of inner desolation — the missing expression of joy signalling total inner collapse
Term names the communal act of praise and thanksgiving directed toward God — the expressive inner orientation of gratitude in worship
Term characterises the person as inwardly glad — the quality of heart that rejoices in God, salvation, or relational flourishing
Term characterises morally negative gladness — the inner glee of the wicked, gladness at another's shame, or perverse delight
Term names the joy that characterises life in God's presence and the celebration of his works — joy as the defining quality of divine worship
Term names joy as the defining inner quality of life before God — the gladness of heart that characterises covenant faithfulness and divine presence
Term names the loss and restoration of inner joy — the absence of gladness that marks desolation and its return that marks divine renewal
Term names rejoicing as the expressive character of renewal and divine creation — joy as the hallmark of eschatological restoration
Term names rejoicing as a state or quality of the inner person — the gladness characterised by or withheld from a particular context or condition
Term names rejoicing as the active inner and communal expression of joy — the gladness that characterises a people, a place, or a relationship
Term names rejoicing as what is taken away in judgment — the inner gladness silenced by divine discipline or catastrophe
Term names exultation as the inner quality characterising the life of the righteous in God's presence and word
Term names the restoration of inner joy as the sign of divine renewal — joy returned to those who were desolate
Term names the inner act of rejoicing in God — the personal and communal joy that arises from trust, salvation, and the knowledge of God
Term names the cosmic and communal rejoicing before the Lord — the earth, creation, and peoples called to exult in divine kingship and justice
Term names the deep inner exultation of the person before God — the soul's leap of joy in salvation, in God's word, and in his presence
Term names divine exultation over his people — God's inner delight and rejoicing that he brings to bear upon his restored community
Term names the inner act of defiant exultation in suffering — the joy that arises from moral integrity before God even in the midst of pain
Term names the inner act of rejoicing before God — the foundational response of the inner person to divine salvation, covenant faithfulness, and presence
Term names morally negative rejoicing — the inner glee at another's fall, the gladness of the wicked, or the perverse delight in evil
Term names divine rejoicing — God's own inner delight and exultation over his people and his works
Term names the inner cheer or comfort whose absence leaves the heart sick with grief — joy as the missing quality that exposes inner suffering
Term names the inner cry of joy and triumph — the ringing shout that expresses inner exultation before God and in his salvation
Term names the inner cry of prayer and petition — the urgent and heartfelt appeal that arises from the depths of the spirit before God
Term names the communal shout of joy or harvest celebration — and its absence as the marker of judgment and inner grief
Term names the communal cry of joy and praise — the expressive inner jubilation voiced by peoples and places in response to divine acts
Term names the spontaneous cry of joy that breaks out in response to a direct divine act — the inner overflow of awe and gladness
Term names the inner act of singing for joy — the vocalized overflow of inner delight and exultation directed toward God or called forth by his acts
Term names the inner state of exultant joy — the surpassing gladness that arises in the presence of or in response to God's salvation
Term names the inner state of gladness — the fullness of joy that fills the heart in God's presence and through his provision
Term names joy as the abiding inner state — the deep gladness that characterises the life of faith and the community of believers
Term names the fullness and completion of joy as the goal of the inner life — the joy that is made complete in God and in his purposes
Term names the inner refreshment and benefit that one derives from another in the Lord — joy as relational nourishment
Term names the inner act of exultant rejoicing — the expressive overflow of deep inner joy, especially before God or in response to his work
Term names the inner act of rejoicing in God, his work, and his people — the abiding orientation of the inner person toward gladness in the Lord
Term names the paradoxical inner joy that persists through and is intensified by suffering, sorrow, and tribulation
Term names the communal sharing of inner joy — the act of rejoicing together in another's good, and the inner orientation of love that makes such shared joy possible
Term names exultation as an inner emotional state of triumphant rejoicing — here the malicious joy of an aggressor who exults in the act of destroying the vulnerable
Term names the wondrous/miraculous as a category of divine self-disclosure — God's identity as wonder-worker and the quality of incomprehensible excellence that is his name
Term names the wondrous works and deeds of God — acts beyond human comprehension that evoke wonder, awe, and praise
Term marks things that surpass human capacity or comprehension — naming an inner-being condition of being exceeded or overwhelmed by reality
Term names the inner state of amazement — a filling of the person with wonder in response to a divine act
Term names the inner act of marvelling — astonishment in response to something remarkable, whether genuine awe or misplaced fascination
Term names marvel or wonder as an inner-being response — astonishment at something remarkable or its absence when deception is recognised
Term names the incomprehensible as a quality of divine identity or knowledge that surpasses human inner capacity — evoking awe at what exceeds the person's grasp
Term designates the wondrous works of God as incomprehensible acts testifying to divine inner perfection — works designed to evoke wonder in the human inner being
Eupsucheō as inner gladness lifted by good news from a beloved person
Ya.tav as the heart being merry or glad
Ya.tav as moral goodness and doing good: inner moral orientation
Ya.tav as inner state of approval or pleasure
Te.ev as exceedingly glad: inner gladness at deliverance through trust in God
Eufrainō as authentic gladness in restoration, hope, and divine presence
Eufrainō as disordered or worldly celebration
Cha.dah as inner rejoicing in Gods saving acts and presence
Term names comfort as God's gift in affliction — the divine consolation that flo
Term names encouragement as the inner orientation toward hope — the exhortation
Term names encouragement as the inner-community act — the mutual comfort of beli
Term names comfort as the inner state grounded in faithfulness to God's word and
Term names consolation as God's gift that cheers the soul amid inner anxiety — t
Term names the tender comfort of love — the personal warmth that soothes the inn
Term names the comfort given by faithful companions — the inner sustenance recei
